annotations.label: Sexagesimal approximation of √2 (diagonal of unit square)

anchor.snippet: an accurate sexagesimal approximation to the square root of 2

annotations.note: Real.sqrt formalizes √2 (the unit-square diagonal), but the specific sexagesimal approximation is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1;24,51,10 as a fraction approximating √2

anchor.snippet: a numerical approximation of the square root of two that is off by less than one part in 2 million

annotations.note: The particular rational approximation 1;24,51,10 of √2 and its error bound are not stated anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 42;25,35 as diagonal of a side-30 square

anchor.snippet: the result of multiplying 30 by the given approximation to the square root of two

annotations.note: This specific numerical product (30·√2 ≈ 42;25,35) is a historical computation with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Alternative interpretation: side equals 1/2

anchor.snippet: one alternative interpretation is that the number on the side of the square is

annotations.note: This is an archaeological interpretation of the tablet, not a mathematical statement formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diagonal of a side-1/2 square (≈0.70711)
annotations.note: The specific decimal approximation of √2/2 ≈ 0.70711 is not formalized, though Real.sqrt would define the underlying value.

annotations.label: Reverse side: 3:4:5 rectangle diagonal

anchor.snippet: a similar problem concerning the diagonal of a rectangle whose two sides and diagonal are in the ratio 3:4:5

annotations.note: PythagoreanTriple captures x*x+y*y=z*z so the 3:4:5 relation is a special case, but the concrete (3,4,5) instance is not a named decl.

annotations.label: Areas of hexagons and heptagons

anchor.snippet: the computations of areas of hexagons and heptagons

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of areas of regular hexagons or heptagons (its 'hexagon' lemmas are category-theory braiding identities).
